title: Carboxylic Acids and Their Darivatives - Chapter Test

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is dimensionless quantity?
questions.solution:
Strain is the only dimensionless quantity among the four.

• Stress has units of force per area:

$$[{\rm Stress}] = \frac{F}{A} = \frac{MLT^{-2}}{L^2} = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Pressure is the same dimensionally as stress:

$$[{\rm Pressure}] = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Force has units:

$$[F] = M\,L\,T^{-2}.$$

• Strain is a ratio of lengths:

$$\text{Strain} = \frac{\Delta L}{L}$$

so all the length dimensions cancel, making it dimensionless.

Answer: Option 2 (Strain).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Bottom of a tank containing water appears to be raised. It is due to
questions.solution:
It is due to Option 2: Refraction of light.

Explanation:

When light rays come from the bottom of the tank and pass from water to air, they bend at the surface because of refraction.

Since the rays bend away from the normal on coming from a denser medium (water) to a rarer medium (air), the bottom appears to be at a smaller depth than its real depth.

So, the bottom of the tank appears raised.

Correct answer:

$$\text{Refraction of light}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Point of Origin of Earthquake Wave is known as
questions.solution:
The point of origin of an earthquake wave is known as the Focus (Option B).

In seismology, the focus of an earthquake refers to the location within the Earth where the rupture of the fault begins, and seismic waves are initially generated. It is beneath the surface of the Earth. The point directly above the focus, on the Earth's surface, is called the epicenter.

Focus: The exact point inside the Earth where an earthquake originates.

Epicenter: The point on the Earth's surface located directly above the focus.

These concepts are fundamental in understanding how energy is released during an earthquake and how it propagates through the Earth's crust.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following colours may be obtained by combining green and red colours?
questions.solution:
Combining green and red colors creates yellow in the RGB color model, which is used based on the additive color mixing process. In this model, the primary colors are red, green, and blue, and the secondary colors are formed by the combination of two primary colors. Here's a breakdown of how the colors combine:

Red + Green results in Yellow.

Green + Blue results in Cyan.

Blue + Red results in Magenta.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option D: Yellow

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following correctly represents the SI unit of resistivity?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following terms cannot represent electrical power in a circuit?
questions.solution:
Electrical power in a circuit can be expressed using different formulas based on the known quantities of voltage (V), current (I), and resistance (R). Let's analyze each option to determine which one is not a valid representation.

Option A: VI

This is a standard formula for electrical power, where power (P) is the product of voltage (V) and current (I):

$$ P = VI $$

Option B: I2 / R

This expression is incorrect. It resembles the formula for power, but power (P) is actually given by:

$$ P = I^2 R $$

The term I2 / R does not correctly represent electrical power.

Option C: I2R

This is another valid formula for electrical power, derived from Ohm's Law. Power (P) can be expressed as:

$$ P = I^2 R $$

Option D: V2 / R

This is also a correct formula for electrical power. By substituting Ohm's Law into the power formula, we get:

$$ P = \frac{V^2}{R} $$

So, the term that cannot represent electrical power is Option B: I2 / R.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The temperature of a body increases  from 310 K to 340 K. The temperature increase in degree Celsius is
questions.solution:
Temperature difference in kelvin and degree Celsius is the same.

Given:

$$ T_1 = 310\ \text{K}, \quad T_2 = 340\ \text{K} $$

Increase in temperature:

$$ \Delta T = T_2 - T_1 = 340 - 310 = 30\ \text{K} $$

So, the increase in degree Celsius is also:

$$ 30^\circ \text{C} $$

Correct option: 2. 30°C

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: We hear an echo due to
questions.solution:
Concept:

Echo: An echo occurs when a sound wave reflects off a surface and is heard a short time after the original sound. This delay is caused by the time it takes for the sound wave to travel to the surface and back.

Reflection of sound: When sound waves encounter a hard surface, they bounce back, producing a reflected sound wave.

Conditions for Echo Formation:

The distance between the sound source and the reflecting surface should be at least 17.2 meters, assuming the speed of sound is approximately 344 m/s.

The reflecting surface should be large and hard to effectively reflect the sound.

Therefore, the phenomenon of hearing an echo is explained by the reflection of sound waves.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The power required to lift a mass of 8⋅0 kg up a vertical distance of 4 m in 2 s is (taking acceleration due to gravity as 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>):
questions.solution:
To determine the power required, we need to follow these steps:

Calculate the work done to lift the mass.

Calculate the power using the time in which the work is done.

Step 1: Calculate the Work Done

Work done $ W $ is given by:

$ W = m \cdot g \cdot h $

where:

$ m $ is the mass ($ 8.0 \, \text{kg} $),

$ g $ is the acceleration due to gravity ($ 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 $),

$ h $ is the height ($ 4 \, \text{m} $).

So,

$ W = 8.0 \, \text{kg} \times 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 \times 4 \, \text{m} $

$ W = 320 \, \text{J} $

Step 2: Calculate the Power

Power $ P $ is given by:

$ P = \frac{W}{t} $

where $ t $ is the time ($ 2 \, \text{s} $).

So,

$ P = \frac{320 \, \text{J}}{2 \, \text{s}} $

$ P = 160 \, \text{W} $

Conclusion

The power required to lift the mass is $ 160 \, \text{W} $. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option B: 160 W

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Pressure has magnitude as well as direction:
As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases
Action and reaction forces act on the same object
Pascal and N/m<sup>2</sup> represent the same unit
questions.solution:
The correct statement is:

$$\text{Option 4: Pascal and } N/m^2 \text{ represent the same unit}$$

Now let us check each option one by one:

  1. Pressure has magnitude as well as direction

    This is wrong because pressure is a scalar quantity.

    It has only magnitude, not direction.

  2. As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases

    This is wrong.

    In a liquid, pressure increases with depth.

  3. Action and reaction forces act on the same object

    This is wrong.

    According to Newton’s third law, action and reaction act on different objects.

  4. Pascal and $N/m^2$ represent the same unit

    This is correct because

$$1 \, \text{pascal} = 1 \, N/m^2$$

So, the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Ram records the odometer readings of his car for the distance covered from 2000 km at the start of his journey and 2400 km at the end of the journey after 8 hours. What is the average speed of the car?
questions.solution:
To find the average speed of Ram's car, we need to use the formula for average speed, which is given by:

$$ \text{Average Speed} = \frac{\text{Total Distance}}{\text{Total Time}} $$

First, let's determine the total distance covered by the car. Ram's car odometer readings at the start and end of the journey are 2000 km and 2400 km respectively. Therefore, the total distance covered is:

$$ \text{Total Distance} = \text{End Odometer Reading} - \text{Start Odometer Reading} $$

$$ \text{Total Distance} = 2400 \, \text{km} - 2000 \, \text{km} $$

$$ \text{Total Distance} = 400 \, \text{km} $$

Next, we are given that the total time taken for the journey is 8 hours.

Now we can calculate the average speed:

$$ \text{Average Speed} = \frac{400 \, \text{km}}{8 \, \text{h}} $$

$$ \text{Average Speed} = 50 \, \text{km/h} $$

Therefore, the average speed of the car is 50 km/h.

The correct option is:

Option A: 50 km/h

The correct answer is:

Option 3: Sound Navigation and Ranging

SONAR is a technique that uses sound waves to detect and locate objects under water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->An astronaut whose weight on the Earth is 600 N experiences weightless- ness on International Space Station orbiting around the Earth. It means that
options: normal reaction of the space- station floor on the astronaut is zero
gravitational pull of earth on the astronaut is zero
space station applies a centrifugal force on the astronaut
questions.solution:
1. On Earth, the weight of the astronaut is

    $$W = mg = 600\text{ N}\quad\bigl(m=\tfrac{W}{g}\bigr).$$

  2. In the International Space Station (ISS) both the station and the astronaut are in continuous free fall around the Earth. They have the same centripetal acceleration towards Earth.

  3. “Weightlessness” means the astronaut feels no contact force from the floor. In free fall:

    – Gravitational force $mg$ acts on the astronaut.

    – There is no normal reaction $N$ from the floor (because both fall together).

  4. Therefore:

    Option 2 is correct – the normal reaction of the space-station floor on the astronaut is zero.

questions.solution:
Since the block is at rest. Therefore

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 10+15=2+15+F \\\\ & \Rightarrow 25=17+F \\\\ & \Rightarrow F=25-17 \\\\ & \therefore F=\mathbf{8} \mathbf{N} \end{aligned} $$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A railway wagon (open at the top) of mass M<sub>1</sub> is moving with speed v<sub>1</sub> along a straight track. As a result of rain, after some time it gets partially filled with water so that the mass of the wagon becomes M<sub>2</sub> and speed becomes v<sub>2</sub>. Taking the rain to be falling vertically and the water stationery inside the wagon, the relation between the two speeds v<sub>1</sub> and v<sub>2</sub> is :
options: $\frac{1}{2}$M1$v^2_1$ < $\frac{1}{2}$ M2$v^2_2$​
M1v1 = M2v2
M1v1 < M2v2
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the problem. Since the rain is falling vertically and the water inside the wagon is stationary, the horizontal momentum of the system (wagon + water) is conserved.

Conservation of Momentum

The initial horizontal momentum of the system is given by: $$ M_1 v_1 $$

The final horizontal momentum of the system, after the wagon has collected water, is: $$ M_2 v_2 $$

According to the principle of conservation of momentum: $$ M_1 v_1 = M_2 v_2 $$

Therefore, the correct relation between the two speeds $ v_1 $ and $ v_2 $ is given by Option C: $$ M_1 v_1 = M_2 v_2 $$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The magnetic field inside a long straight solenoid-carrying current
options: decreases as we move towards its end
increases as we move towards its end
questions.solution:
The magnetic field in an ideal, infinitely long, tightly‐wound solenoid is the same everywhere inside. In fact, by Ampère’s law one finds

$$B \,=\, \mu_0\,n\,I$$

where

• μ₀ is the permeability of free space,

• n is the number of turns per unit length, and

• I is the current through the winding.

Because this expression has no dependence on the position along the axis, the field is uniform inside. (Near the very ends of a real, finite solenoid the field does fall off, but for a “long” solenoid those edge effects are negligible.)

Answer: Option 4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the nature of velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration?
questions.solution:
The nature of the velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration is a straight line. This is because uniform acceleration means that the velocity of the car changes at a constant rate over time.

Mathematically, if a car is moving with uniform acceleration, the relationship between its velocity $ v $ and time $ t $ can be described by the equation:

$$ v = u + at $$

Where:

  • $ v $ is the final velocity.
  • $ u $ is the initial velocity.
  • $ a $ is the constant acceleration.
  • $ t $ is the time.

This equation is of the form of a linear equation $ y = mx + c $, where $ m $ (the slope) represents the acceleration $ a $, and $ c $ (the y-intercept) represents the initial velocity $ u $. Therefore, the graph of velocity versus time will be a straight line.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C: Straight line

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: If the speed of light in carbon disulfide and vacuum is XXXXX X X and YYYYY Y Y respectively, then
options: X < Y
X > Y
X ≥ Y
X = Y
questions.solution:
Light travels slower in a medium than in vacuum because its refractive index $n>1$. For carbon disulfide ($n_{\rm CS_2}\approx1.63$) we have

speed in CS₂: $X = \dfrac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}}$

speed in vacuum: $Y = c$

Since $n_{\rm CS_2}>1$,

$$X = \frac{c}{n_{\rm CS_2}} < c = Y\,. $$

Answer: Option 1, $X < Y$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following diagrams may correctly represent the motion of a skydiver during a jump ?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

How a Skydiver Falls

Step 1: Skydiver Jumps

When a skydiver jumps out, gravity pulls them down. Their speed goes up fast at first because they are falling freely through the air.

Step 2: Reaching Terminal Velocity

As the skydiver falls faster, air pushes back harder. Eventually, the push from the air (air resistance) matches the pull of gravity, so the skydiver's speed stops increasing. This steady speed is called terminal velocity.

Step 3: Opening the Parachute

When the parachute opens, there is a sudden big increase in air resistance. This makes the skydiver slow down very quickly.

Speed-Time Graph:

The speed-time graph starts by going up quickly (as the skydiver speeds up). Then, it flattens out (terminal velocity). After the parachute opens, the line drops sharply as the skydiver slows down fast.

EXPLANATION:

Option 1:

This graph is correct. It shows speed going up fast, staying the same for a while (terminal velocity), and then dropping quickly after the parachute opens.

Option 2:

This option is incorrect because it makes the speed go to zero too fast, which doesn't happen in real skydiving.

Option 3:

This option is wrong because it shows speed going up and down in a straight line, as if the skydiver stops suddenly without a parachute. This is unrealistic.

Option 4:

This option almost matches what really happens, but it does not clearly show a flat part for terminal velocity and the slowing down isn't sudden enough when the parachute opens.

So, Option 1 is correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A body sinks in water when
options: density of water is greater than density of body
density of water is less than density of body
density of water is equal to density of body
questions.solution:
A body sinks in water when its density is greater than the density of water.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 2: density of water is less than density of body

Reason:

According to the NCERT concept of flotation:

  • If density of body $>$ density of liquid, the body sinks

  • If density of body $<$ density of liquid, the body floats

  • If density of body $=$ density of liquid, the body may remain suspended

Here, for sinking in water:

$$ \text{Density of body} > \text{Density of water} $$

which means

$$ \text{Density of water} < \text{Density of body} $$

Hence, Option 2 is correct.
